source: https://www.origincoffee.co.uk/blogs/journal/kalita-wave-brew-guide and aggregated roaster guides; flat-bottom 3-hole Kalita gives even extraction, fuller body and enhanced sweetness vs V60, slightly less clarity; generic median cup, no cupping score
embedding_text: Kalita Wave flat bottom three hole pour over percolation even extraction balanced sweet fuller body medium clarity forgiving vs V60 cone

source: https://kurasu.kyoto/blogs/kurasu-journal/kalita-wave-brewing-guide-how-we-do-it-at-kurasu ; 15g/250g 1:16.67, 90-93C, EK43 ~7.5 medium grind, bloom 30g, pours 120g@1:10 then 100g@1:45, total 2:30-2:45; described nectar-like and silky; flavor qualitative not cupped
embedding_text: Kurasu Kalita Wave recipe 15g 250g 1:16.67 medium grind three pours nectar silky sweet balanced percolation

source: https://kaldiscoffee.com/blogs/recipes/kalita-wave-185-pour-over-how-to-beginner ; 20g/320g 1:16, medium grind, ~95-96C, bloom 40-60g then steady spiral to 320g, total 2:30-3:00; flat-bottom drives up sweetness, balanced cup accentuating chocolate nutty dried-fruit; flavor qualitative
embedding_text: Kaldi Kalita Wave 185 beginner recipe 20g 320g 1:16 medium grind balanced sweet chocolate nutty dried fruit flat bottom percolation

source: https://coffeechronicler.com/hario-v60-vs-kalita-wave/ and https://www.steampunkcoffee.co.uk/blogs/steampunk-coffee-blog/comparing-the-kalita-wave-and-the-v60 ; three smaller drain holes add flow restriction limiting localized over-extraction, flat bed + wave filter pushes water away from walls; more uniform forgiving cup with clarity and elegance, slightly less acidity-driven complexity than V60; no fixed recipe given
embedding_text: Kalita Wave vs V60 three drain holes flow restriction flat bed wave filter even uniform forgiving cup clarity elegance less channeling percolation
